title: Rankett context handoff — separating internal tool from white-label
html:
TL;DR

  • A short Jan 6 self-orientation note: Yahir tells his coding agent to load (1) the Jan 4 session-handoff doc and (2) the rankett.com PDF as context.
  • Names the architectural tension explicitly — the Rankett internal tool and the white label surface need to share the same engine but split into two front-doors with their own portals/logins.
  • Reads as a moment of "we have been cooking in a manner that scares me" — pace anxiety from the Codex/Claude-Code solo founder loop.
  • Sits one day after the bigger Jan 5 Partner Program manifesto — this is the orientation handoff for the next coding session.
  • Triangulates against 2026 01 05 ISSUES TONIGHT and the broader whitelabel strategy thread.

Notes

The note is barely a paragraph but it captures the structural moment cleanly — Yahir realizes the engine he is building has to serve two simultaneously-real audiences with non-overlapping UIs (Rankett-as-internal-fulfillment vs. agency-as-resold-product). The recurrence of "like we have discussed this" tells you Yahir's working memory has slid into the agent's context window — he is briefing the model the way he'd brief a co-founder. Pairs cleanly with the Diddles call where the same architectural split was first verbalized aloud.

Open questions

  • Was Jan 4 session-handoff doc itself ever ingested? Worth grepping next densify.
  • The "rankett.com PDF" referenced — same as the mid Jan junk drawer?
